name: date-picker
description: Date input composed from Popover + Calendar. Shows the selected date formatted via date-fns, opens a calendar grid on click.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback when the user selects a date: (date: Date | undefined) => void

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Text shown on the trigger when no date is selected

props.name: dateFormat
props.description: date-fns format token for the trigger label (e.g. 'PPP', 'yyyy-MM-dd')

props.name: aria-label
props.description: Accessible label — required when no adjacent visible <label> is used

examples.title: Basic date picker
examples.description: Bind a Date state and render the picker

ai.whenToUse: Use for selecting a single date in a form. Shows a formatted text label and opens a month grid on click. Composes Popover + Calendar + button trigger.
ai.whenNotToUse: Don't use for date ranges (compose Calendar mode='range' + Popover yourself). Don't use for native mobile date UX (<input type='date'> is often better on phones). Don't use if you need time selection — combine with a separate time picker.
commonMistakes: Passing a string to value — must be a Date object
Missing aria-label when the picker has no adjacent visible <label>
Overriding className in a way that hurts focus ring visibility
Forgetting that the popover auto-closes on select — provide onChange to capture the value
ai.accessibilityNotes: Trigger is a real <button> with focus ring. When rendered without a visible label, pass aria-label. The popover portals and traps keyboard focus inside Calendar until the user selects or presses Escape.
